title: Dense Shattering Middle-Eastern Porcini Mujadara
description: Indulge in this shattering, tangy, and comforting Middle Eastern dish featuring porcini and a shio-koji-cure, all dairy-free-friendly, served in a slate tile. Perfect for a special occasion or dinner party.

ingredients.name: Porcini mushrooms
ingredients.notes: fresh or dried, rehydrated according to package instructions

ingredients.name: Shio-koji cure
ingredients.notes: store-bought or homemade, used to cure the short ribs

ingredients.name: Short ribs
ingredients.notes: beef or lamb, bone-in for added flavor

ingredients.name: Rice
ingredients.notes: short-grain or medium-grain, rinsed and drained

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: minced, added for depth of flavor

instructions: Season the short ribs with salt, black pepper, and shio-koji cure, letting them cure for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Caramelize the onion and garlic in olive oil until golden brown and fragrant.
Add the rehydrated porcini mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown.
Add the rice and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes.
Transfer the mixture to a slate tile and create a well in the center.
Add the short ribs to the well and cover with the rice mixture.
Transfer the slate tile to the preheated oven and cook for 25-30 minutes, or until the short ribs are tender and the rice is cooked through.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
